description_long: This is a vintage, weathered lantern with a cylindrical metal body and a rounded base, showing signs of rust and patina. The lantern features a prominent glass enclosure in the center, protected by a wire mesh and flanked by two sturdy, curved metal arms that connect the base to the top. A metal handle arches over the top, allowing for easy carrying or hanging, and a small latch mechanism is visible on one side for accessing the interior. The top section is vented, likely to allow heat and smoke to escape when the lantern is in use. The overall design is utilitarian, with a rugged, industrial appearance typical of oil or kerosene lanterns used in outdoor or urban settings. The surface is predominantly dark green with patches of rust and wear, giving it an aged, authentic look.
description: A weathered, vintage metal lantern with a cylindrical glass enclosure, wire mesh protection, and a curved carrying handle. The surface is dark green with visible rust and patina, giving it an aged, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass enclosure, wire mesh, and latch mechanism clearly, with both curved arms and the handle visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the metal arms and the profile of the handle, with the wire mesh and glass enclosure partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front but without the latch mechanism, showing the symmetry of the arms and the full glass enclosure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, showing the handle and arm profile, with the wire mesh and glass enclosure visible from a different angle.
